methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Black Semiconductor, a Series A chip design company founded by Daniel and Sebastian Schall, is evaluated against a peer set of 4 same-stage semiconductor companies (Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, Rain AI) alongside a broader public comparables universe of 3 large-cap semiconductor names (ALAB, NVDA, AMD). The company has raised €254.4M to date.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable valuation technique. Four Series A semiconductor peers exhibit valuations ranging from $500M (Rain AI) to $5.0B (Etched), with a median of $1.5B. This method directly anchors the valuation given Black Semiconductor's identical stage and sector positioning. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Black Semiconductor's revenue is not disclosed; while the public semiconductor universe trades at a 24.2x price-to-sales median, absence of target revenue precludes meaningful application.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.3B to $1.7B, with a median of $1.5B supported by the peer-median approach. Key uncertainties include Black Semiconductor's revenue trajectory relative to peers, product differentiation versus the wide valuation dispersion ($500M to $5.0B) observed in the comparables set, and timing of inflection toward profitability or customer traction. The single-method reliance reflects data constraints typical of early-stage private semiconductor companies.
